topic: Controlling Story Pace with Sentences and Punctuation
title: Controlling Pace: How Sentence Length and Commas Build Your Story

questions.question: What does "pace" mean?
options: A part of a sentence that gives extra information but cannot stand alone as a complete sentence (e.g., 'As soon as Eager arrived').
A punctuation mark (,) used to separate parts of a sentence, such as items in a list or extra information.
A feeling of excitement, suspense, or worry that makes you wonder what will happen next.
The speed at which a story unfolds; how fast or slow things happen.
questions.correct_answer: The speed at which a story unfolds; how fast or slow things happen.
questions.explanation: "pace" means: The speed at which a story unfolds; how fast or slow things happen.

questions.question: What does "tension" mean?
options: A part of a sentence that gives extra information but cannot stand alone as a complete sentence (e.g., 'As soon as Eager arrived').
A punctuation mark (,) used to separate parts of a sentence, such as items in a list or extra information.
A feeling of excitement, suspense, or worry that makes you wonder what will happen next.
The speed at which a story unfolds; how fast or slow things happen.
questions.correct_answer: A feeling of excitement, suspense, or worry that makes you wonder what will happen next.
questions.explanation: "tension" means: A feeling of excitement, suspense, or worry that makes you wonder what will happen next.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Vary your sentence lengths to control the pace and ______ tension in your story.
questions.explanation: The full sentence is: "Vary your sentence lengths to control the pace and create tension in your story."

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about this topic?
options: Vary your sentence lengths to control the pace and create tension in your story.
Commas are important punctuation marks that help organise sentences and make them clear.
Short sentences can speed up the action; longer sentences can slow it down and add detail.
Use commas to separate items in a list, mark subordinate clauses, and enclose parenthetical (extra) information.
questions.correct_answer: Short sentences can speed up the action; longer sentences can slow it down and add detail.
questions.explanation: Correct! "Short sentences can speed up the action; longer sentences can slow it down and add detail." is one of the key things to remember.
